# Daniil Makeev - AI Engineer | Generative Systems | RAG

> AI Engineer focused on generative pipelines, RAG systems, and production-grade automation. I build AI products that reduce manual work, improve answer quality, and turn LLM prototypes into reliable systems.

**Canonical URL**: https://wyddy.tech/

## About

**Daniil Makeev** | AI Engineer

- **Location**: Moscow, Russia
- **Focus**: Generative systems, RAG assistants, and production AI automation
- **Education**: Bauman Moscow State Technical University, Computer Science and Engineering
- **Status**: Incomplete higher education, graduation expected in 2026

## Experience

### Hao (https://hao.vc)
**AI Engineer / Lead AI Generation Engineer** | January 2025 - Present

Built video generation pipelines and a contact-centric RAG assistant for production AI workflows.

- Automated script generation workflows and cut manual work roughly in half.
- Parallelized generation flows and sped up per-unit content processing by 3x.
- Implemented LLM request deduplication and reduced generation costs by about 48%.
- Added reranking to contact search reasoning, improving answer relevance by about 60%.
- Introduced profile personalization and increased LTV by about 34%.
- Isolated user data with RLS to eliminate cross-user data exposure risks.

**Stack**: RAG · pgvector · PydanticAI · GenAI · ElevenLabs · deepagents

### Zenit-Elektro Metrology Company
**Python Developer** | June 2023 - January 2025

Owned internal Python services focused on ETL and data quality.

- Built an ETL pipeline that automated manual operations and cut site moderation time by 3x.
- Integrated data validation that reduced import errors by about 60%.

**Stack**: Python · FastAPI · asyncio · HTTP · ETL · PostgreSQL

## Selected Projects

### NetWho (AI CRM Network Assistant)
Bot built on aiogram 3.x with RAG architecture. Implemented semantic contact search with vector search and news parsing via Jina AI. Data validation with Pydantic v2.

[Live Demo](@netwho_bot) — [Community](t.me/netwho)

### Video Generation Pipelines
Built automated generation pipelines for video content. Focus areas: orchestration, prompt workflows, queueing, deduplication, and flexible scene assembly.
